I treated the prompt format as fixed and the 45 prior turns as a proposal log, not as proof. The prompt requires the first two generators to be the tame generators ŌÉ,ŌĄ, with ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2 implicit, and later generators in the pro-2 core; the prior log‚Äôs only serious unrefuted candidate is still the dynamic tame-inertia word from Turns 9‚Äď45.

The live candidate remains:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ŌÄ=ŌÄ2 and ő∑=2ŌÄ on odd tame inertia,r=xŌÉ‚ąíŌÄxŌÉŌÄx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ ((yŌĄ)ŌÄ)ŌÉy‚ąí1,where ŌÄ‚Č°40491355905,‚ąíŌÄ‚Č°45176306175,‚ąíő∑‚Č°50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of ő∑ is immaterial in this word because ŌĄ has prime-to-2 finite tame images.The first thing to fix is a repeated algebraic error in the recent turns. The correct sign-cover Fox coefficient is not the one printed in several of the last ‚ÄúESTABLISHED‚ÄĚ blocks. Let V=kerőļ be the sign double cover, t its deck involution, and work on a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block ŌĄ‚Ü¶őĪÓÄ†=1. ForNa (u)=(uŌĄa)ŌÄ,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0‚ąĎŌÄ‚ąí1(tőĪa)j.

Since (1‚ąítőĪa)Da=1‚ąí(tőĪa)ŌÄ and (tőĪa)ŌÄ=t, the correct formula isDa=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1.This is the only direction that makes the unit/radical argument legitimate: 1‚ąítőĪa is a 2-adic unit, while 1‚ąít is radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient ofx‚ąí1(xŌĄ‚ąíő∑)ŌÄ is ‚ąí1+D‚ąíő∑, a unit. So the desired conclusion survives: nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots. But the usable formula is (1‚ąít)/(1‚ąítőĪa), not its reciprocal.The trivial tame-inertia elimination also needs a stable convention. With T the ŌÉ-cycle operator and s‚Č°ŌÄ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+T‚ąís)X+(1+T)Y=0.

If f=2a m with m odd, then s‚Č°1(mod2a) and s‚Č°0(modm). For f even, s is odd, so 1+T‚ą£1+T‚ąís. Thus the legitimate elimination isY=Ps (T)X+YňČ,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís,up to replacing T by T‚ąí1. The reciprocal is not legitimate in F2 [T]/(Tf‚ąí1). With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(T‚ąí1),one obtains, for a nontrivial f-th root ő∂,(1+ő∂)h(ő∂)=ő∂‚ąís+ő∂s+1=ő∂‚ąís(1+ő∂2s+1).Since 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero, and h(1)=1. The prior nonsingularity conclusion is therefore sound, but only with this direction of division.With these two corrected formulas, the prior abelianization checks remain consistent:Ue,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,and for the sign kernel,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4.The torsion is entirely in the tame-inertia-invariant block; the nontrivial odd tame-character blocks are unit-pivot blocks.My new computation this turn is a reduction of the first marked IA ambiguity problem. LetőĒe,f=‚ü®ŌÉňČ,ŌĄňČ‚ą£ŌĄňČe=1,ŌÉňČf=1,ŌÉňČŌĄňČŌÉňČ‚ąí1=ŌĄňČ2‚ü©,where e is odd and e‚ą£2f‚ąí1. Write f=2a m with m odd, let

H=‚ü®ŌĄňČ,ŌÉňČ2a‚ü©be the odd Hall subgroup, and P=őĒ/H‚ČÉC2a. In the reduced degree-one DemuŇ°kin model,L1‚ČÉF2 [őĒ]‚äēF2 b‚äēF2S,with marked S=ŌÉf, andq=g‚ąąőĒ‚ąĎXg[2]+[b,S].ThenL2=(L1[2]‚äē‚ąß2L1)/‚ü®q‚ü©.The first residual marked IA ambiguity after odd-Hall rigidity isAe,f=H1 (P,HomH(F2 [őĒ]‚äēF2b,L2)).

The useful reduction is:Ae,f‚ČÖH1(P,L2H).

Reason: HomH(F2 [őĒ],L2) is coinduced/free as a P-module, so its H1 (P,‚ąí) vanishes by the finite 2-group Shapiro argument. The only contribution comes from the trivial b-summand, giving HomH(F2b,L2)=L2H.

I then recomputed L2H as an F2 [P]-module. Writing Jr for a unipotent Jordan block of size r for the generator of P, the results are:(e,f)(1,2)(3,2)(3,4)(5,4)(15,4)PC2C2C4C4C4L2H decompositionJ13‚äēJ23J15‚äēJ24J12‚äēJ2‚äēJ48J12‚äēJ25‚äēJ410J12‚äēJ25‚äēJ430dimAe,f35377

For a cyclic 2-group P=C2a, each nonfree indecomposable block Jr with r<2a contributes one dimension to H1 (P,‚ąí), and each free J2a contributes none. This explains why (5,4) and (15,4) both have a 7-dimensional ambiguity despite very different kernel ranks: the extra regular J4 -blocks are cohomologically invisible.This sharpens Turn 45‚Äôs ‚Äúdimension 7‚ÄĚ observation. The ambient Hom space is huge, but the actual obstruction lives in the small nonfree part of L2H. Therefore the next arithmetic comparison should not try to compare an enormous operator matrix. It should extract the candidate‚Äôs class in the seven nonfree J1/J2 directions of L2H, then compare those directions with Kummer/Witt arithmetic for the tame degree-20 field.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I corrected the two recurring reciprocal errors:Da=(1‚ąít)(1‚ąítőĪa)‚ąí1,Ps(T)=1+T1+T‚ąís (up to T‚ÜĒT‚ąí1). With these corrections, the prior conclusionsUe,fab‚ČÖZ2ef+1‚äēC2,(kerőļ)ab‚ČÖZ22ef+1‚äēC4 remain consistent. New this turn: the first marked IA ambiguity reduces toAe,f‚ČÖH1(P,L2H),and for (e,f)=(5,4),L2H‚ČÉJ12‚äēJ25‚äēJ410,so dimA5,4=7.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient is a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidate‚Äôs exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Compute the candidate‚Äôs actual class in the seven nonfree J1/J2 directions of L2H for (e,f)=(5,4), then compare those seven coordinates with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic for the corresponding tame degree-20 extension.[BREAKTHROUGH]
